title: Bees Make Homes in Old Animal Bones
summary: Long ago on an island called Hispaniola, a big owl caught a rodent called a hutia and took it to a cave. The owl ate the hutia, leaving its bones on the cave floor. Later, a bee came looking for a place to build its nest. It found the hutia's jawbone, which had small holes where teeth used to be. The bee used these holes as a perfect home. More bees came and did the same thing. Scientists found these bones with bee nests inside them in a cave in the Dominican Republic. They were careful when digging up the bones and noticed the smooth holes. At first, they thought wasps made the nests, but they learned it was bees. This discovery shows how bees can use old bones to live in.

keywords.explanation: a flying insect that makes honey and builds nests
a type of rodent that lived on an island
a big hole in the ground or rock where animals can live
hard parts inside animals that give them shape
a home that animals like bees build to live in
people who study things to learn new facts

options: In a cave in the Dominican Republic

Why did the bee choose the hutia's jawbone for its nest?
options: Because the holes were the right size

questions.question: What did scientists do carefully when they found the bones?

background_read: This story is about a discovery in a cave where bees made nests in old animal bones. It happened a long time ago on an island in the Caribbean. Scientists study fossils, which are old bones turned to stone, to learn about past life. Bees are insects that often build nests in holes, but using bones is very rare. This helps us understand how animals adapt to their environment.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists like Lazaro Viñola Lopez and bees
WHAT: Bees built nests in fossilized hutia bones in a cave
WHERE: In the Cueva de Mono cave in the Dominican Republic on Hispaniola island
WHY: To show how bees can use unusual materials for homes and how careful observation leads to discoveries

perspectives.description: Finding bee nests in bones helps scientists learn about ancient ecosystems and animal behavior.
